key_insight: Human and LLM-generated synthesis recipes performed similarly, and their closed-loop collaboration enabled discovery of a new material, Ba3PtO5.
engineering_relevance: This shows AI can accelerate materials synthesis planning, but experimental validation remains essential for turning predicted materials into real discoveries.
method: Generated synthesis recipes for known and unknown Ruddlesden-Popper candidates with both humans and LLMs.
Tested each recipe in the lab and fed the outcomes back into a closed-loop collaboration.
Compared success rates across two rounds to assess learning and improvement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports similar lab success rates for humans and LLMs, with high performance on known materials and much lower performance on unknown targets across both rounds.
watch_item: Results depend on a narrow materials family and lab feedback loop, so generalization to other chemistries and broader synthesis settings may be limited.
paper_title: Human and LLM Collaboration for Accelerated Materials Synthesis and Discovery
